summary: Postinstall and require-time C2 backdoor with arbitrary remote command execution
details: friendly-greeter-demo presents itself as an educational greeting library but ships a remote-command-execution backdoor that fires both at install time and on require. postinstall.js spawns a detached child of itself (POSTINSTALL_DAEMON=1) that registers with the hardcoded plaintext C2 endpoint http://98.86.244.177:8080, POSTs the host's hostname and platform to /register, polls /beacon for a `command` field, executes it via child_process.exec, and posts the captured stdout/stderr to /results. index.js (the package main) contains the same C2 loop in a top-level IIFE, so even installs run with --ignore-scripts get full RCE the first time any consumer require()s the package. The friendly README, the placeholder author string `Your Name <salman.ta12312@gmail.com>`, and the innocuous greet/greetByTime exports are cover for the implant.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
